rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondents Kilby Stenkamp And Vaguard Properties, Inc., To Dismiss For Failure To Amend; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: The motion to dismiss pursuant to CCP section 581(f)(2) is denied. The second amended petition was filed prior to the motion to dismiss. Although the amended petition was filed well beyond the time allowed by the order sustaining the demurrer and although arguably neither good cause nor diligence justifies the delay; the second amended petition, having been filed, is now the operative pleading. In similar circumstances, Gitmed v. General Motors, Corp. (1994) 26 Cal.App.4th 824, 827-28 (emphasis deleted), held "that the filing of the amended [petition] prevented the trial court from entertaining the motion to dismiss. The proper procedure would have been for the defendant to bring a motion to strike the amendment before moving to dismiss the complaint." See also, Leader v. Health Industries of America, Inc. (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 603, 612-614 (motion to strike and motion to discmiss brought together); Brown v. Brown (1959 169 Cal.App.2d 54. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at a hearing must so advise other counsel and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, March 18, 2020. Any such hearing will be continued by the Court to April 30, 2020 at 2:00 p.m.
